commit.message:
refactor(rhyme): remove extractLineTail from barrel exports (#516)

* refactor(rhyme): remove extractLineTail re-exports from barrels

extractLineTail is @deprecated in lyricSegmenter.ts v3.1.
Remove it from both barrel exports so no new import can reference
the shim through the public API surface.

The shim itself remains in lyricSegmenter.ts (compat) until all
call sites in tests are migrated.

* fix(test): add __mocks__ stubs for @upstash/ratelimit and @upstash/redis

Vitest resolves vi.mock() targets at module graph time.
@upstash/ratelimit and @upstash/redis are optional runtime-only deps
(dynamic import in _rateLimit.ts) — never added to package.json.
Without stubs the module resolver throws before the mock factory runs,
causing the entire test suite to fail.

Stubs expose the minimal API exercised by _rateLimit.test.ts:
- Ratelimit class with slidingWindow() + limit()
- Redis class (constructor only)

* fix(tsconfig): exclude __mocks__ from tsc compilation

__mocks__/@upstash stubs are Vitest-only artifacts.
tsc picks them up by default (no explicit include) and fails
because @upstash/* types are not in node_modules.
Adding __mocks__ to exclude keeps verify green.

* fix(test): move @upstash stubs to api/__mocks__ (Vitest manual mock convention)

Vitest resolves manual mocks relative to the file under test, not the
project root. __mocks__ at root is a Jest-only convention.
Moving stubs to api/__mocks__ ensures vi.mock('@upstash/ratelimit')
and vi.mock('@upstash/redis') resolve correctly when Vitest runs
api/_rateLimit.test.ts.

* chore: remove root __mocks__ (moved to api/__mocks__)

* chore: remove root __mocks__ (moved to api/__mocks__)

* fix: 3 pre-existing CI test failures blocking refactor/remove-extractLineTail-reexport (#518)

* Initial plan

* fix: resolve 3 failing CI tests and bump version to 1.12.0.37

- TopRibbon.test.tsx: replace .toHaveAttribute() with .getAttribute() to
  avoid dependency on jest-dom matchers not loaded in Vitest setup
- App.tsx: read isGenerating from useComposerContext() instead of
  hardcoding false, so AppShell receives the correct generating state
- package.json: bump version from 1.11.0.37 to 1.12.0.37

commit.message:
feat: add local Ollama fallback mechanism with automatic cloud-to-local switching

- Implement dual-endpoint AI brain supporting cloud models (gpt-oss:120b, deepseek-v3.1, mistral-large-3) and local fallback (gemma4:e2b)
- Add local_fallback_enabled, local_fallback_model, local_base_url config parameters
- Enhance AIBrain.__init__() to initialize both cloud and local Ollama clients
- Implement automatic fallback: tries all cloud models, then switches to local on failure (rate limit, auth, timeout)
- Add check_connection() logic to test cloud first, fall back to local if needed
- Create _call_ollama_local() method for local model inference
- Update model_used field in AIDecision to track which model/endpoint was used
- All tests pass: config loading, initialization, cloud/local connection, model selection logic
- When cloud Ollama API rate-limits (429) or auth fails, bot automatically uses local gemma4:e2b
- Tested and verified: cloud detection works, fallback is automatic, local model produces valid decisions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(TOP/tb): resolve arbiter3 concurrent bit-select race

Root cause: logic [2:0] src_valid is a packed vector. When three
forked threads concurrently write src_valid[0], [1], [2] with
blocking assignments, each does a read-modify-write on the same
variable. In Xcelium, the last writer wins, clearing bits set by
earlier threads. This prevents some FIFOs from receiving their
packets, so pkt_available never reaches 3'b111.

Fix: Change src_valid from packed logic [2:0] to unpacked logic [3]
(same as src_data). Each element is a separate variable, eliminating
the concurrent r-m-w race. Also add 2-cycle settle after fork/join
for packet_count_q to register the last EOC.

TB issue, NOT an RTL bug.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(data): sync master slug to slugByLocale.it for 297 jobs

13 crawlers had jobs where job.slug (master, serves IT URL path)
diverged from slugByLocale.it (the properly translated slug).
This caused the Italian URL to serve a stale/untranslated slug.

For each fixed job:
- job.slug synced to slugByLocale.it
- Old master captured in previousSlugsByLocale.it for bridge pages
- Old master added to legacy previousSlugs array

Affected crawlers: coop-ticino (130), coop-ticino-locale-cache (130),
eoc (11), ferrovia-retica (5), casale (4), denner (4), hilcona (3),
otis (2), posta-svizzera (2), pwc (2), skyguide (2), aldi (1),
julius-baer (1).

fix: add window.__CSRF__ pattern for updated Passport HTML (#9)

* fix: add window.__CSRF__ pattern for updated Passport HTML

Yandex Passport now renders the CSRF token as:

    window.__CSRF__ = "value";

instead of the previous `csrf_token` input/JS/JSON formats. The existing
three patterns all match empty strings on the new page, causing
`CsrfExtractionError`.

Add a fourth pattern to `CSRF_PATTERNS` that matches the new
`window.__CSRF__` global assignment. Existing patterns are preserved for
backward compatibility.

- Add `re.compile(r'window\.__CSRF__\s*=\s*\"([^\"]+)\"\')`
  to `CSRF_PATTERNS` in `constants.py`
- Add `tests/fixtures/csrf_window_global.html` fixture
- Extend `test_csrf_patterns` parametrize to cover the new pattern

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(score): add Agent Impact Score (0-100) per project

Computes a composite score measuring AI coding agent fingerprint:
- Throughput surge (0-25): lines/dev + PR rate growth vs baseline
- Slop signal (0-40): rejection rate + first-time contributor increase
- Review anomaly (0-20): TTM decrease while volume grows
- Consistency (0-15): how many months show the pattern

Confidence levels: low/medium/high based on data availability.

* fix(SM-16): address all PR #764 review findings

1. Eager callableIndex — maintained on add() like classByName/implByName,

Add pvnode als alternative Provider (#256)

* Add pvnode forecast service integration

Add pvnode (pvnode.com) as a fourth forecast provider, based on
the SOLECTRUS forecast-collector implementation. pvnode provides
high-resolution PV forecasts with 15-minute intervals.

Changes:
- New lib/pvnode.js converter module (API response to standard format)
- main.js: pvnode URL building, Bearer auth, response parsing
- admin/jsonConfig.json: pvnode service option, forecast days config
- io-package.json: pvnodeForecastDays default config
- i18n translations for all 11 languages
- Updated German and English documentation

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

* Add pvnode paid/free account handling and extra parameters

- Add pvnodePaid checkbox to distinguish free/paid accounts
  - Free: forecast_days fixed at 1 (today + tomorrow)
  - Paid: forecast_days configurable up to 7
- Add pvnodeExtraParams text field for optional API parameters
  (e.g. diffuse_radiation_model, snow_slide_coefficient, shading_config)
- Forecast days field only visible when paid account is enabled
- Updated all 11 i18n translations and documentation

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

* Add pvnode clearsky, temperature, and weather_code to JSON output

pvnode provides additional data beyond power forecasts. These fields
are now extracted from the API response and included in the per-plant
JSONData and JSONTable output:

- watts_clearsky: clear-sky reference power (W) per timestamp
- temperature: temperature (°C) per timestamp
- weather_code: WMO weather code per timestamp

JSONData entries for pvnode now include: t, y, clearsky, temp, weather_code
JSONTable rows for pvnode now include: Time, Power, Clearsky, Temperature, WeatherCode

Based on SOLECTRUS forecast-collector pvnode_adapter.rb implementation.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

* Add pvnode summary clearsky and request batching with second_array

- Summary JSONData and JSONTable now include summed clearsky values across
  all plants (temperature and weather_code excluded from summary as they
  cannot be meaningfully summed)
- Implement pvnode request batching using second_array API feature: up to
  2 plants per API request, reducing API call count by ~50% (critical for
  free accounts with 40 requests/month limit)
- Extract pvnode fetch logic into dedicated updatePvnodeServiceData method
- Extract shared error handling into handleServiceError method
- Update documentation (EN/DE) with batching and summary notes

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

* Add temperature and weather_code to pvnode summary JSON (first value wins)

Since all plants share the same location, temperature and weather_code
are identical across plants. These values are now included in summary
JSONData and JSONTable using a first-value-wins approach (no addition),
matching the SOLECTRUS accumulate pattern.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

* fix: correct pvnode summary docs - temperature and weather code are included

The documentation incorrectly stated that temperature and weather code
are only available per plant, not in the summary. In fact, the code
does include them in the summary (using the first plant's values).

https://claude.ai/code/session_01NtLi589RGwbhYbpxS93ncc

pvnode API returns timestamps in UTC. Without explicit UTC parsing,
moment.js treated them as local time, causing a timezone offset

Use moment.utc().local() to correctly parse as UTC and convert

healthcheck: add network-connectivity log check for supervised *arr apps

Prowlarr (and other *arr apps) can lose internet connectivity while the
process continues running, leaving the container incorrectly marked healthy.

Adds two new functions:
- check_app_logs: scans a log file for error patterns within a configurable
  time window (APP_LOG_CHECK_MINUTES, default 5). Uses awk to extract lines
  from the window — including multi-line .NET stack trace continuation lines
  — then greps for each pattern. Returns 1 (unhealthy) on first match.
- check_app_specific: dispatches to check_app_logs for supervised apps
  (jackett, lidarr, prowlarr, radarr, readarr, sonarr) using
  /config/supervisord.log and a set of network-specific patterns.

Pattern rationale:
  SocketException          root .NET transport exception; DNS, connect,
                           EAGAIN, unreachable — almost always network
  Resource temporarily unavailable  EAGAIN (errno 11) — user's exact case
  Network is unreachable   ENETUNREACH (errno 101)
  No route to host         EHOSTUNREACH (errno 113)
  Name or service not known  DNS resolution failure
  Connection timed out     ETIMEDOUT (errno 110)
  HttpClient.Timeout       TaskCanceledException from HTTP client timeout;
                           constrained to avoid matching routine cancellations

Bare HttpRequestException and WebException are intentionally excluded:
they also trigger on 4xx/5xx protocol errors and auth rejections that do
not indicate a network outage.

commit.message:
Document that recursive file search requires --recursive flag

Updated docstring for resolve_latest_rvtools_file() to clarify that
only the immediate directory is searched. Recursive multi-host search
happens when --recursive flag is used, which calls resolve_recursive_rvtools_files()
to find per-host .xlsx files organized in subdirectories (e.g., vcenter_prod/).

This allows:
- Without --recursive: Single report from latest file in root directory
- With --recursive: Separate reports from each hostname subdirectory
